title: run podman in container raise "Error: no such file or directory"
body:
### Issue Description

related issue : #16304

When run podman in a guix container, `podman COMMAND` raise "Error: no such file or directory"

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. in shell, run
```
$ guix shell --container --network podman   # launch the container from host system
[env]$ podman --log-level trace info # in the container, run podman
```

### Describe the results you received
```
[env]$ podman --log-level trace info    
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level trace          
DEBU[0000] Called info.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level trace info) 
Error: no such file or directory
```
### Describe the results you expected
normal shell output
### podman info output
`podman info` no runnable
```yaml
$ uname -a
Linux localhost.localdomain 6.0.9 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC 1 x86_64 GNU/Linux
[env]$ podman --version
podman version 4.2.1
```



### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless


### Upstream Latest Release

No, latest release doesn't still exist in my distribution

### Additional environment details

podman is run in a guix container with only  the podman package and its dependancies installed:
`guix shell --container --network podman`

### Additional information

podman CLI without command argument is runnable
```
[env]$ podman --version
podman version 4.2.1
[env]$ podman 
Manage pods, containers and images

Usage:
  podman [options] [command]
...
Error: missing command 'podman COMMAND'
```

comments.body:
I am not familiar with guix, running podman inside a container is complicated because it requires extra privileges.

I suggest you run this under strace -f to see what exactly causes the `no such file or directory` error

```
$ strace -f podman info 2> bug.log
$ cat bug.log | grep "No such file or directory"
access("/etc/ld.so.preload", R_OK)      = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
openat(AT_FDCWD, "/sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/hpage_pmd_size", O_RDONLY) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] openat(AT_FDCWD, "/etc/localtime", O_RDONLY) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] statfs("/sys/fs/cgroup/unified", 0xc00071f578) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)

[pid     6] newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/tmp/containers-user-1000/containers", 0xc0004d56f8, 0) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/dev_1/.config/containers/storage.conf", 0xc0004d5898, 0) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] <... newfstatat resumed>0xc0004d5d78, AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/dev_1/.config/containers/policy.json", 0xc0004d5f18, 0) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/etc/containers/policy.json", 0xc0006b2378, 0) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)

[pid     6] newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/dev_1/.config/containers/containers.conf.d", 0xc00061b628, AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/dev_1/.config/containers/containers.conf", 0xc000a0aed8, 0) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
[pid     6] statfs("/sys/fs/cgroup", 0xc0005b15e8) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)

```

The container is very minimal and basic, podman doesn't run maybe because the container miss few system file.

I guess it is failing because `/sys/fs/cgroup` is missing?
@giuseppe Could podman even work without mounted cgroup?

never tried that.

Would it work if you specify `--cgroups=disabled`?

I can't find this option in `podman --help`.

I can only see the option follow in `podman --help`:
```' --cgroup-manager string       Cgroup manager to use ("cgroupfs"|"systemd") (default "cgroupfs")```

`strace -f podman run --cgroups=disabled docker.io/pdf2htmlex/pdf2htmlex 2> bug2.log` give same log.

`--cgroup-manager systemd` seem allow bypass the issue:
```
# start container
dev_1@localhost ~ [env]$ sh -c "echo $(id -un):100000:200000 >> /etc/subuid"
dev_1@localhost ~ [env]$ sh -c "echo dev_1:100000:200000 >> /etc/subgid"
dev_1@localhost ~ [env]$ podman info --cgroup-manager systemd
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "vfs" overwritten by graph driver "overlay" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve.  May prevent use of images created by other tools 
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
cannot setresgid: Invalid argument
Error: setting up the process
```
note: I haven't systemd package installed in the container
